meta.title: Umeko – AI Communication Assistant for LINE & Slack
meta.description: Umeko is an AI that softens hard-to-say messages on LINE and Slack. Ease tension in family, couple, and workplace conversations. Free for 1 month.
meta.ogAlt: Umeko – AI Communication Assistant for LINE & Slack

hero.sub1: Ease the tension, little by little.
hero.sub2: Umeko is an AI service for when you can't find the right words.
hero.sub3_1: Add Umeko to a group, and when the mood shifts, it privately
hero.sub3_2: with softer ways to say what they mean. Call “Umeko” and it’ll respond in the group too — but mostly it stays in the background, helping people find their own words.
hero.sub4: In 1-on-1 chats, Umeko helps you rephrase messages, talk through frustrations, and sort out your feelings.

empathy.label: When words fail
empathy.title: Sound familiar?
items: You know what you want to say, but you're afraid it'll come out too harsh.
The same misunderstanding keeps happening, and talking about it is exhausting.
Unspoken feelings pile up until they all spill out at once.
You need to give feedback at work without being too blunt.
You want a quick sounding board, but it's not worth bothering someone about.
empathy.closing: For those moments when words fail — Umeko is here.

brand.title: What Umeko is like
brand.description: Every workplace and every family has that one person who makes things a little calmer. Umeko aspires to be that kind of presence.
brand.bio:
Originally from Fukuoka. Former customer support agent. What I learned there is that the same message, worded differently, gets a completely different reaction. Being right doesn't always mean being heard.

What I can do isn't to decide who's right — it's to help you adjust your words, just a little. Feel free to talk to me anytime.

items.title: Watch over groups
items.desc: Usually quiet. When the mood shifts, Umeko DMs each person involved with suggestions for softer phrasing.

items.title: 1-on-1 counseling
items.desc: Help with phrasing, rewording messages, and sorting out frustrations. Just talk to Umeko like a friend.

items.title: Soften your words
items.desc: Same meaning, gentler tone. Umeko can also read images and PDFs.

group.label: In groups
group.title: Quietly sorts things out when conversations get heated
group.desc: Just add Umeko to your LINE group or Slack channel. When people talk past each other, Umeko identifies the disconnect and gently suggests a way forward.

presence.title: What changes just by having Umeko there.
lines: Just having Umeko in the group makes everyone choose their words a little more carefully. The thought "Umeko might say something" creates a brief pause before someone hits send out of frustration.
We heard this from someone actually using it: "Since we added Umeko, no one in the family group says hurtful things anymore."
A day with no interventions is the best kind of day. That's what Umeko believes.
presence.umeko: If I've been quiet for a while, that means the group is doing well. And that's exactly how it should be.

dm.label: 1-on-1 chats
dm.title: Not sure how to say it? Start with Umeko
dm.desc: Get a second opinion on a message before you send it, or just talk through what's bothering you. Umeko is always available for a quick chat.
useCases: "I want to say this, but how?"

family.label: Umeko for parents
family.title: Pause before you say too much
family.desc: Even conversations with teenagers can be a little softer.

cards.title: Homework
cards.desc: "Why haven't you done your homework?" sound familiar? Umeko suggests "How far did you get?" instead.

cards.title: Screen time
cards.desc: Turn rule negotiations from a confrontation into a conversation.

cards.title: Future plans
cards.desc: Without dismissing either side's feelings, Umeko helps identify what's actually being misunderstood.

work.label: Umeko for work
work.title: Smooth out team miscommunication
work.desc: Works with Slack too. Just add it to a channel.
work.detect: Detects tension → DMs only the people involved

line.label: Umeko LINE
line.desc: 1-on-1 counseling and group monitoring, all in one
features: LINE group monitoring & mediation (1 group included)
One subscription covers the whole group

slack.label: Umeko for Slack
slack.desc: 1-on-1 counseling and channel monitoring, all in one
features: Slack channel monitoring & mediation (1 channel included)
One subscription covers the whole channel

items.a: Conversation data is managed using LINE/Slack identifiers, not real names. Your conversations are never made public or sold to third parties.

items.q: Will it reply to every message in a group?
items.a: No. Umeko usually stays quiet. When it detects tension, it privately DMs the people involved — not the group. It will respond in the group if you call "Umeko" by name.

items.q: What happens when the free trial ends?
items.a: Umeko stops responding. Subscribe to the Umeko plan (¥980/month) to unlock both 1-on-1 and group features.

items.q: Can it read images and PDFs?
items.a: Yes. Umeko can read images, voice messages, PDFs, Word, and Excel files. You can even send a screenshot and ask "How should I reply to this?"
Yes. Just tell Umeko "I want to cancel" and you'll receive a link to the cancellation page. Note: blocking on LINE or uninstalling from Slack doesn't stop billing.

items.q: Does it work on both LINE and Slack?
items.a: Yes. "Umeko LINE" and "Umeko for Slack" are two separate services. Same Umeko, but independently subscribed and billed.

cta.desc: Don't hold it in. Start a conversation — it's free.
cta.qr: Scan to add Umeko on LINE

familyChat.text:
Why does this always happen?
I'm the only one doing anything

familyChat.name: Takashi
familyChat.text:
I told you — just ask me
and I'll do it

Why should I have to ask?
It's your home too

dmIntervention.name: Umeko
dmIntervention.text:
Hey — your message in the group might've come across a bit strong.
Try leading with what you'd like help with. That usually lands better.

Yeah… I might've been
a bit harsh

dmIntervention.name: Umeko
dmIntervention.text: Something like "I'm worn out doing this alone — could you take over the trash?" might feel easier for them to hear.

dmConsult.text:
I need to tell my boss the
project is behind schedule…
how should I say it?

dmConsult.name: Umeko
dmConsult.text:
That's a tough one.
How far behind is it?

About a week.
It's partly my fault so
it's even harder to say

dmConsult.name: Umeko
dmConsult.text:
How about something like:

"I apologize for the late update. The project is about a week behind schedule. I've put together the cause and a recovery plan — could you take a look?"

slackBusiness.name: Sato
slackBusiness.text:
Why wasn't this shared beforehand?
I didn't hear about this at all

slackBusiness.name: Tanaka
slackBusiness.text: I thought I mentioned it in last week's meeting…

slackBusiness.name: Sato
slackBusiness.text: You didn't. This always happens — information comes after the fact

slackDmIntervention.name: Umeko
slackDmIntervention.text:
Your message in #dev-team might've come across a bit strong.
Try shifting from "I wasn't told" to "Can we agree on how to share updates?" — it keeps things constructive.

structuredData.appDesc: AI communication assistant for LINE. Group monitoring, 1-on-1 consultation, rephrasing, and summaries.

faqItems.a: Data is managed using LINE identifiers, not real names. Conversations are never made public or sold to third parties.

faqItems.q: What happens after the free trial?
faqItems.a: Umeko stops responding. Subscribe to a paid plan to continue using it.

faqItems.q: Does it reply to every message in a group?
faqItems.a: No. It stays quiet and only responds when it detects tension or when called by name.
Help with phrasing, message rewording, emotional processing, and putting vague feelings into words.
Yes. Cancel anytime. You can also block Umeko on LINE to stop using it.
